verificationLabel: Official NRL round wrap checked Monday 13 Apr 2026
summary: Round 6 finished as a split tipping week at 4/8, but the charity bet stayed perfect with the Cowboys getting home and the running profit moving to $11.50.

sources.label: Friday Footy: Dragons v Sea Eagles, Broncos v Cowboys
Saturday Session: Rabbitohs v Raiders, Sharks v Roosters, Storm v Warriors
Sunday Session: Eels v Titans, Wests Tigers v Knights

scoreboard.note: The live round has already produced an upset. The original board had Panthers as the highest-confidence tip.
One of the cleaner reads on the card and an early recovery after the Panthers miss.
TAB screenshot confirms Cowboys head to head at 2.00 won, with the $5.00 stake collecting $10.00.
The Raiders pinched a close one and flipped what looked like a reasonable Souths read.
The volatile pick still got home and helped stop the round from tipping fully negative.
The Warriors made a huge statement and Melbourne can no longer be treated as a safe rebound side.
The Titans blew Parramatta off the park and turned the Eels into the harshest miss on the card.
The Tigers fully justified the hype and remain one of the strongest stories of the season.

liveAlerts.text: Cowboys beat Brisbane 35-31 and the Round 6 charity bet landed at 2.00, returning $10.00 from the $5.00 stake.
The running charity profit is now $11.50 after back-to-back wins.
Round 6 finished 4 from 8 after misses on Panthers, Rabbitohs, Storm and Eels.
Sea Eagles 28-18 Dragons, Roosters 34-22 Sharks and Tigers 42-22 Knights were the other successful reads.
Warriors 38-14 Storm and Titans 52-10 Eels were the big form resets coming out of the weekend.
Round 7 should start with upgraded respect for the Tigers and Warriors, and downgraded trust in Storm and Eels bounce-back narratives.
